revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:彦島八幡宮]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}}|image=File:Hikoshima Hachimangu Rōmon - 2020.jpg | name = Hikoshima Hachimangu | location = 5-12-9 Sakomachi, Hikoshima, Shimonoseki, Yamaguchi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.941639|N|130.895889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Chūai|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}; {{ill|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}; {{ill|Nintoku|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Ōjin|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | status = Former [[Ken-sha|Prefectural Shrine]] | established = October 1159 (Heiji 1)<ref name="yuisho">[https://hikoshima-guu.net/smarts/index/20/ History] - Hikoshima Hachimangu</ref> | main_building = | alternate_names = | pilgrimages = | annual_festival = Closest weekend to October 21 | ceremonies = | map = {{location map|Japan Yamaguchi|lat_deg=33|lat_min=56|lat_sec=29.9|lon_deg=130|lon_min=53|lon_sec=45.2|label=Hikoshima Hachimangu|position=right|width=230|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=230|coord={{coord|33|56|29.9|N|130|53|45.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|1=Shimonoseki}} }}
'''Hikoshima Hachimangu''' (ひこしまはちまんぐう) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hikoshima|en|Hikoshima|qid=Q11288918|lt=Hikoshima|1=Hikoshima}}, {{ill|Shimonoseki|en|Shimonoseki|qid=Q203308|lt=Shimonoseki|1=Shimonoseki}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|qid=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It is the guardian shrine of Hikoshima<ref>[https://shimonoseki-kgb.jp/spot/彦島八幡宮/ Hikoshima Hachimangu] - Shimonoseki Tourist Guidebook</ref>.
== History == In October 1159 (Heiji 1), the shrine was established by Kōno Michitsugu, who retrieved a bronze mirror (goshintai) depicting Hachiman Bosatsu from the sea and enshrined it after being invited from [[Usa Jingū|Usa Shrine]]<ref name="yuisho"/>. The scene is reenacted during the "Saiagari Shinto Ritual," a traditional ceremony held during the annual autumn festival in October, which is recognized as an intangible folk cultural property of Shimonoseki City<ref>[https://shimonoseki-kgb.jp/events/サイ上り神事/ Saiagari Shinto Ritual] - Shimonoseki Tourist Guidebook</ref><ref>[https://yamaguchi-tourism.jp/spot/detail_15308.html Saiagari Shinto Ritual] - Yamaguchi Prefecture Tourism Site</ref>. In 1349 (Shōhei 4), the shrine was relocated to its current location by Michitsugu's descendant Michihisa<ref name="yuisho"/>.
The shrine has been revered by those involved in shipbuilding and fishing, and it is also worshipped as a deity for safe childbirth under the name "Koyasu Hachiman"<ref name="yuisho"/><ref>[https://www.hikoshima.jp/彦島散策へgo/彦島八幡宮/ Hikoshima Hachimangu] - Hikoshima District Community Development Council</ref>.
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
== Grounds == * main hall and Worship Hall * Zuihōden * Monument commemorating Emperor Chūai's temporary stay ** Yumi-tate no Matsu (Pine Tree where the Bow was Raised) * Sacred Rock (Iwakura) and Petroglyph * Romon (Tower Gate) * Guardian Lion-Dogs (Komainu) * Chōzuya (Purification Pavilion) * Sacred Pond and Sacred Bridge * Torii Gate
; Sub-shrines * Ōtoshi Shrine * Wakamiya Shrine * Suijin Shrine
== External Shrines and Associated Shrines == The shrine is responsible for various shrines around the Hikoshima area<ref>[https://hikoshima-guu.net/smarts/index/18/ Hikoshima Tour] - Hikoshima Hachimangu</ref>. * Takenoko Island Kotohira Shrine * Takenoko Island Tenmangu Shrine * Kazaotomari Ebisu Shrine * Amago Ebisu Shrine * Kibune Shrine * Funajima Shrine ({{ill|Ganryū-jima|en|Ganryū-jima|qid=Q909675|lt=Ganryū-jima|1=Ganryū-jima}}) * Shiogama Shrine * Fukuura Kotohira Shrine * Tano no Kubihachiman Shrine * [[Rokuren-jima]] Hachiman Shrine ** Kōjin and Seven Shrines
== festivals == * January 1 - [[Saitan-sai]] (New Year festival) and First Drum ceremony * January 3 - Genshi-sai * Second Monday of January - Coming-of-Age festival * February 3 - [[Setsubun-sai]] and Tsuina-shiki * February 11 - Kigen-sai and National Foundation Day Celebration * February 17 - [[Kinensai]] (Prayers for a Good Harvest) * February 23 - Tenchō-sai * March 15 - Kazaotomari Ebisu Shrine festival * First weekend in April - Takenoko Island Kotohira Shrine festival * April 9 - Rokuren-jima Hachiman Shrine Kōjin festival * Mid-April Saturday - Funajima Shrine festival and {{ill|Sasaki Kojirō|en|Sasaki Kojirō|qid=Q549737|lt=Sasaki Kojirō|1=Sasaki Kojirō}} Memorial Service * April 15 - Hikoshima District War Dead Memorial Service * May 5 - Children's festival * Early May holiday - Shiogama Shrine festival * Third weekend in May - Fukuura Kotohira Shrine festival * June 10 - Amago Ebisu Shrine festival * Late June - Supporters' Association General Meeting * June 30 - [[Ōharae]] (Great Purification) * July 9 - Rokuren-jima Hachiman Shrine Seven Shrines festival * July 15 - Takenoko Island Tenmangu Shrine festival * July 29-30 - Natsu-koshi festival * September 22-23 - Kibune Shrine festival * September 23 - Autumnal Equinox festival and Autumn Ancestral festival * October 4-5 - Rokuren-jima Hachiman Shrine festival * Second weekend in October - Tano no Kubihachiman Shrine festival * Third weekend in October - annual Grand festival * November 3 - Meiji-sai * November 15 - [[Shichi-Go-San]] * November 23 - [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] (Harvest festival) * December 31 - Ōharae and {{ill|Joya|en|Joya|qid=Q2515816|lt=Joya|1=Joya}} festival
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://hikoshima-guu.net/ Hikoshima Hachimangu] - Official Website * {{Instagram|hikoshimahachimangu.offcial}}
